chunks.text: I visited the home of the teacher who had guided me in my university years.
He was the person whom students of my generation had respected most.
He lived in an old house which had a quiet garden.
The figure who appeared at the door looked much more mature than before
yet his eyes were the same as long ago.
In his study, thousands of books stood on the shelves.
Among them were some manuscript that he himself had written.
He recommended one book to me, saying
" This is the book which I wanted you to read when you were young.
" The book that I had wanted to read turned out to be exactly this one.
He served me tea with the same gentleness that he had shown when I was his student.
What I discovered was a bundle of letters from scholars with whom he had been corresponding for many years.
Those letters were the source of his wisdom.
